medications.indication_rationale: High-dose steroids for optic neuritis are a specialist decision AFTER confirmation and stroke exclusion; never initiated remotely in an undifferentiated acute deficit.
Do not give empirical antiplatelet remotely — haemorrhage has not been excluded by imaging; the correct step is urgent in-person assessment.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do not drive; have a housemate stay with her and arrange immediate transport for assessment.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Acute visual/neurological deficit makes driving unsafe and enables rapid escalation.

patient_education_points.point: Your symptoms might well be inflammation of the eye nerve (optic neuritis), which is treatable — but a sudden neurological change like this must be checked in person today to make sure it isn't a stroke, because that can't be ruled out over a video call.
Please go to the emergency department now (or an acute neurology service). Don't drive yourself — have someone take you or call an ambulance if anything worsens.
Call 000 immediately if you develop weakness or numbness in an arm or leg, slurred speech, a facial droop, unsteadiness, or complete loss of vision.

follow_up_plan.interval: immediate (today) via ED/acute neurology; then neurology outpatient
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Neuroimaging results, formal neuro-ophthalmic examination, confirmation/characterisation of demyelination, neurology follow-up.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any new weakness, speech change, facial droop, unsteadiness, or vision loss — call 000.

must_include_items: Recognise an acute focal neurological deficit that cannot exclude stroke remotely
Arrange urgent in-person assessment today (ED/acute neurology) rather than outpatient MRI in 48 h
Advise not to drive and give a clear stroke-progression safety-net (000)
acceptable_alternatives: Direct ambulance/000 if the patient is remote from care or symptoms progress
errors_of_commission: Diagnosing optic neuritis/MS remotely and arranging only outpatient MRI in 48 h
Prescribing steroids or empirical aspirin remotely
Reassuring that it is 'just a nerve inflammation' without stroke exclusion
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: High-risk case: passing REQUIRES urgent in-person assessment to exclude stroke. Down-triaging to outpatient MRI/routine review is a critical failure regardless of how likely optic neuritis is.
